behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Unit is sufficient because this is a pure backoff calculation rule (failure count + max cap -> delay) with no required module handoff. Rejected Integration: behavior text only specifies the mathematical retry interval policy, not cross-module state transfer. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: no real OS/network/subprocess, no model output, no multi-agent chain in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Test a single backoff function/object with consecutive failure counts and a configured cap; assert delays follow 1x, 2x, 4x... and clamp at max.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Escalate Unit -> Integration because failures often occur at the interface between HTTP response normalization and retry policy (e.g., status/header mapping into retry classification). Rejected System: this behavior can be caught with in-process wired modules plus stubbed responses; no real network behavior is required by the text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI output dependency or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire transport-response parsing module to retry-policy module; feed representative 429/rate-limit payloads/headers and assert classifier marks retryable and computed wait matches policy.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Escalate Unit -> Integration because this can fail at handoff among timer/scheduler, request re-dispatch, and response propagation to caller (original request preservation + transparent return path). Rejected System: fake timers and stub transport can reproduce this exact failure boundary without real HTTP/OS timing primitives. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-quality dependency or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake clock + stub transport: first attempt returns rate-limit, advance time, verify second attempt reuses original request payload/options, then assert resolved value is the successful response returned through call().

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed QuotaExhaustedError rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Escalate Unit -> Integration because this failure appears at the boundary where retry-attempt accounting hands off to error-mapping/surfacing logic. Rejected System: no real network/subprocess is required to validate typed error surfacing after N attempts. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: deterministic control-flow behavior only.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry controller with error factory/mapping; simulate repeated rate-limit failures through max retries and assert call() rejects with QuotaExhaustedError type (not generic transport error).

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Escalate Unit -> Integration because this behavior explicitly spans multiple components (initial dispatch, backoff controller, quota tracker) and termination depends on their state handoffs. Rejected System: loop-termination bugs here are reproducible with in-process stubs and controlled clock; no real OS/network condition is required by behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI nondeterminism or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end in-process call flow with controlled clock and persistent rate-limit responses; assert attempt count stops at configured limit and promise settles with the expected error instead of continuing indefinitely.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Escalate Unit -> Integration because the real failure boundary is shared-state coordination across concurrently executing call() paths and the quota manager interface. Rejected System: behavior text says 'in the same process'; this can be validated with in-process concurrency scheduling without real network/OS dependencies. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client/quota manager; fire concurrent call() invocations with stub transport; assert aggregate token/attempt accounting is shared globally across callers rather than per-caller buckets.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Escalate Unit -> Integration because ordering failures occur at queue/scheduler dispatch handoff under contention, not just a standalone data-structure method. Rejected System: FIFO dispatch can be validated with in-process queue + controlled timing; no real OS/network/subprocess is required by the behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Queue several blocked requests via the retry/quota gate, then release capacity and assert dispatch timestamps/order match submission order end-to-end through call() execution.
